name: Schools
description_short: GreatSchools is a national nonprofit providing free school ratings, reviews, and parenting resources to help families find and evaluate K-12 schools across the U.S.
description_long:
GreatSchools has operated for over 25 years as a trusted school search and information platform serving millions of families. Founded as a national nonprofit, the organization aggregates and analyzes education data from all 50 state departments of education, the District of Columbia, and federal sources to create comprehensive profiles on 150,000+ public, charter, and private schools nationwide. The platform positions itself as the nation's most trusted school search resource for education information and decision-making support.

GreatSchools offers free school ratings and reviews, a searchable database of schools by location and type (elementary, middle, high, K-12), guided search tools to match schools to family priorities, school comparison features, and a library of parenting resources. Users can search schools, read and write reviews, follow schools for updates, and create comparison lists. Schools and districts can claim their profiles to add enrollment application links and respond to community reviews. The platform also provides data licensing and marketing solutions for educational institutions.

The organization distinguishes itself through comprehensive government data aggregation, a community-driven review system with over 200,000 contributors, free access to all core features for families, and a dual focus on supporting both parents and schools. Their mission explicitly addresses educational equity and helping communities diminish inequities in education, positioning the service beyond simple school shopping.

As a nonprofit operating primarily through free services and partnerships, GreatSchools does not provide financial products, credit services, or emergency funding. While the platform collects substantial user data through cookies and analytics, this is standard for large web platforms. The resource is limited to K-12 school information and parenting guidance—it does not address higher education, trade schools, or career training beyond its school database scope.
services: Free school search database by location and school type (elementary, middle, high, K-12)
Guided search tool matching schools to family priorities and curriculum preferences
School comparison tool for side-by-side rating and feature review
School ratings based on state and federal education data
Community school reviews and ratings system
Email notifications and school-following feature for enrollment updates
School profile claiming and management for administrators
Parenting resources library with age-appropriate guidance
Research reports on education trends and equity
Data and media solutions licensing for schools and partners
Marketing solutions for schools and districts to reach families
pros: Completely free access to school ratings, reviews, and comparison tools for all families
Coverage of 150,000+ schools across all 50 states plus D.C., including public, charter, and private institutions
Data sourced directly from state departments of education and federal government for accuracy
Community-driven review system with over 200,000 active contributors providing real parent perspectives
Guided search feature helps families prioritize school selection criteria beyond test scores
Schools can claim profiles to provide accurate, current enrollment and program information
Comprehensive parenting resource library supporting child development at every stage
Nonprofit mission explicitly focused on reducing educational inequities, not maximizing profits
cons: School ratings are limited to government-reported metrics and may not capture school quality comprehensively
Reviews are user-submitted and unverified, so quality and bias vary significantly
Website collects extensive user data through cookies and tracking for advertising purposes despite nonprofit status
Does not address affordability barriers or financial aid options for private school selection
Limited scope to K-12 only; does not help families explore higher education, vocational, or alternative pathways
best_for: Families searching for schools in their area and wanting to compare options before enrollment decisions
Parents seeking peer reviews and community feedback about specific schools
Families looking for free parenting resources and child development guidance aligned with school selection
School administrators and districts wanting to manage their public profile and respond to parent feedback
diagnosis: GreatSchools is essential for families navigating school selection and seeking free, community-verified information about local K-12 options. The main caveat is that it provides education information and parenting support, not financial assistance—families facing enrollment barriers due to cost should explore separate resources for tuition aid, vouchers, or charter school funding options.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: GreatSchools is a national nonprofit providing free school ratings, reviews, and parenting resources for K-12 families.
